title: Scope B T5: baseline filename unification `${id}--${slug}.md` + auto-migration in fab scan
description: Change baseline KB emit path from `${entry.slug}.md` to `${entry.id}--${entry.slug}.md` (matches promoted-entry format). Add pre-scan `migrateLegacyBaselineFilenames(target)` that scans canonical knowledge dirs for `.md` files NOT matching `KT-[A-Z]+-\d+--.*\.md`, reads each frontmatter `id:`, and if id is in the known baseline id allowlist hardcoded in scan.ts, unlinks the old file and writes the new name. Updates content_ref construction at scan.ts:1278. One `fab scan` completes the migration end-to-end.

files.change: Change `join(target, KNOWLEDGE_DIR, entry.target_subdir, \`${entry.slug}.md\`)` to `join(target, KNOWLEDGE_DIR, entry.target_subdir, \`${entry.id}--${entry.slug}.md\`)`. Note: entry.id is allocated earlier so it's available here in the rendered MarkdownEntry.
Mirror the filename change: content_ref becomes `${KNOWLEDGE_DIR}/${entry.target_subdir}/${entry.id}--${entry.slug}.md`.

files.target: new function migrateLegacyBaselineFilenames + call site in runInitScan
files.change: Add new fn `async function migrateLegacyBaselineFilenames(target: string): Promise<{ migrated: Array<{ from: string; to: string; id: string }> }>`. Hardcode KNOWN_BASELINE_IDS = ['KT-MOD-0001','KT-MOD-0002','KT-MOD-0003','KT-PRO-0001','KT-GLD-0001'] + ci-config id when present. For each canonical KB subdir, readdir; for each `.md` file whose name does NOT match `/^KT-[A-Z]+-\d+--.*\.md$/`, read frontmatter, extract id, if id is in KNOWN_BASELINE_IDS + sluggified-name reasonably matches the entry slug -> rename via unlink + atomicWriteText with new content (preserves content; only filename changes). Call this fn at the start of runInitScan, before the candidate-build loop. Side-effect: also update sidecar (.scan-state.json) key entries if they reference the old path.

Replace bare-slug filename assertions (e.g., expect 'code-style.md') with id-prefixed form (e.g., expect 'KT-MOD-0001--code-style.md' or whichever id the deterministic allocator assigns). Update all 6-7 builder tests that assert filename.

Update assertions on resulting files in .fabric/knowledge/* and agents.meta.json content_ref entries to expect new format. Add new test 'migrates_bare_slug_to_id_prefixed' that pre-seeds a bare-slug baseline file with frontmatter id matching KNOWN_BASELINE_IDS, runs init scan, then asserts old path is unlinked and new id-prefixed path exists with same content.

implementation: Add migrateLegacyBaselineFilenames fn near top of scan.ts with KNOWN_BASELINE_IDS hardcoded set
Wire migration call as first step of runInitScan (before forensic read or right after, but before entry build loop)
Update line 171 + line 1278 emit paths to id-prefixed format
Update scan-builders.test.ts filename assertions across 6-7 builders
Add scan-init.test.ts migration test covering: pre-seeded bare-slug file with matching id → renamed; bare-slug file with unknown id → left alone (NOT renamed); already-id-prefixed file → no-op
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/cli test scan` to confirm green

reference.pattern: Existing baseline emit at scan.ts:170-190 + sidecar update flow at scan.ts:181-193
reference.examples: Use atomicWriteText (already imported) + readFile for migration writes; mirror existing readForensic helper for frontmatter parsing if no helper exists yet

criteria: All baseline files written by `fab scan` use `${id}--${slug}.md` filename format
agents.meta.json content_ref entries reflect new filename format after scan
Pre-seeded bare-slug baseline files with id in KNOWN_BASELINE_IDS are auto-renamed in one `fab scan` run
Bare-slug files with id NOT in allowlist (e.g., user-promoted KP-* files) are left untouched
scan-builders.test.ts + scan-init.test.ts pass with new assertions
Migration covers all 4 currently-existing legacy bare-slug baselines on this repo (code-style.md, tech-stack.md, module-structure.md, readme-first-paragraph.md)

rationale.chosen_approach: Migration auto-runs inside fab scan; zero extra command, hardcoded allowlist prevents accidental touching of user entries
decision_factors: fab scan is already the canonical baseline-emit command
rationale.tradeoffs: Hardcoded allowlist needs update if new baseline slot added later (acceptable; small surface)

manual_checks: Pre-seed a bare-slug file in `.fabric/knowledge/guidelines/` matching KT-GLD-0001, run fab scan, observe rename
success_metrics: Repo-level `fab scan` dogfood produces clean id-prefixed canonical layout
